questions.scenario: Checkout failure rate rises after traffic moves to `orders-api--v31`. The old revision `orders-api--v30` is still healthy and can receive traffic. What is the safest first recovery action?

options.text: Route traffic back to the known healthy revision and verify recovery.
Delete the old revision so no one accidentally uses it.
Increase traffic to the new revision to collect more evidence faster.
Grant broad storage permissions before checking which dependency failed.

questions.explanation: When user harm is clear and a rollback target is healthy, returning traffic to that target limits impact.

questions.scenario: A release changed both the image and `RECEIPTS_STORAGE_ACCOUNT`. Routing traffic to the old revision does not fix receipt uploads. What did the rollback plan miss?

options.text: Configuration rollback for the changed setting.
A second health check path with the same logic.
A new resource group for the storage account.

questions.explanation: Rollback must restore every release-critical change, not only the code or revision.

questions.scenario: Which facts make a rollback decision safer?

options.text: The previous slot or revision is named.
The team knows which config values changed.
The evidence that proves recovery is defined.
The deployment command was copied from an old incident.

questions.explanation: Rollback needs a target, scope, and proof. Reusing an old command without current evidence is risky.
